title: cordia lineata
intro: Cordia lineata, also known as the beechwood tree or the wild plum, is a medicinal plant species that belongs to the Boraginaceae family. The plant is valued for its leaves, bark, and roots, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cordia lineata include treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as having antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in folk medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
